name: Clouds
description: Granular texture synthesizer. Captures stereo audio into a buffer and generates overlapping grains with variable pitch, position, size, density. Four alternate modes accessed via hidden combos.

controls.name: Freeze
controls.description: Freezes the audio buffer so new audio stops being written. All grains continue scrubbing the last-captured material.
controls.detail: Button lit = frozen · Gate input also triggers freeze

controls.name: Position
controls.description: Playhead location within the buffer. CCW = newest audio, CW = oldest. Sweeping scrolls backwards through captured material.
controls.detail: Bipolar attenuverter for CV · 0–100% of buffer length

controls.name: Size
controls.description: Grain duration. Small = percussive/metallic, large = time-stretched/blurred. Audio-rate modulation creates FM-like artifacts.
controls.detail: ~1ms to ~1s · bipolar attenuverter · audio-rate CV allowed

controls.name: Pitch
controls.description: Transposes grain playback ±2 octaves. CCW = down, noon = unity, CW = up. V/OCT tracked.

controls.name: Density
controls.description: Grain rate. CCW from noon = rarer grains with space; CW from noon = overlapping cloud. Below 9 o'clock produces Poisson (random) triggering.
controls.detail: Below noon: random/Poisson · above noon: regular/synchronous · CV bipolar

controls.name: Texture
controls.description: Grain envelope shape. CCW = boxcar/Tukey (crisp), CW = Gaussian (smooth). Also adjusts grain diffusion window in blend mode.
controls.detail: Bipolar attenuverter · interacts with blend parameters in alternate modes

controls.name: Blend / Spread / Feedback / Reverb
controls.description: Single knob with four selectable functions chosen by short-pressing the small Quality button: dry/wet blend, stereo spread, feedback amount, reverb amount. The Blend LED color indicates the currently selected assignment.
controls.detail: Short-press Quality cycles slot: green=Dry/Wet · red=Spread · orange=Feedback · yellow=Reverb (holding FREEZE + short-pressing Quality instead cycles the four alternate algorithms)

controls.name: In Gain
controls.description: Input trim for left/right audio inputs. Red LED on peak indicates clipping into the buffer.
controls.detail: Unity at noon · +24dB CW · peak LED ~0dBFS

inputs.name: FREEZE
inputs.description: Gate input. High = buffer frozen, low = recording. Complements front panel button.

inputs.name: TRIG
inputs.description: Trigger input. In granular mode gates a single grain. In alternate modes doubles as clock/event input.

inputs.name: POSITION CV
inputs.description: CV for buffer position, scaled by attenuverter.

inputs.name: SIZE CV
inputs.description: CV for grain size; accepts audio-rate input.

inputs.name: BLEND CV
inputs.description: CV for whichever blend slot (dry/wet, spread, feedback, reverb) is currently selected.

inputs.name: IN L / IN R
inputs.description: Stereo audio inputs. Left normalized to right for mono operation.
inputs.normalizedTo: IN L feeds IN R when unpatched

outputs.name: OUT L / OUT R
outputs.description: Stereo audio outputs of the granular engine (or selected alternate mode).

behaviors.name: Granular mode (default)
behaviors.description: Continuous stereo grain cloud reading from the rolling buffer. POSITION scans, SIZE sets grain length, DENSITY sets rate, PITCH transposes. Blend knob accesses dry/wet, spread, feedback, reverb.

behaviors.name: Pitch-Shifter / Time-Stretcher mode
behaviors.trigger: hold FREEZE + short-press Quality button until Blend LED is yellow
behaviors.description: Time-stretching / pitch-shifting algorithm. DENSITY becomes playback-speed control, SIZE becomes window/overlap. POSITION and PITCH decouple — you can hold pitch while stretching.

behaviors.name: Looping Delay mode
behaviors.trigger: hold FREEZE + short-press Quality button until Blend LED is red
behaviors.description: Classic pitch-shifting delay with feedback. SIZE sets delay time up to ~8s (mono) / ~4s (stereo). POSITION scans within the delay buffer to create tape-style scrubbing.

behaviors.name: Spectral Madness mode
behaviors.trigger: hold FREEZE + short-press Quality button until Blend LED is pink
behaviors.description: FFT-based spectral mangler. POSITION scans frequency bins, SIZE changes window length, DENSITY and TEXTURE perform magnitude clipping and phase randomization. Produces paul-stretch-like drones and phaser effects.

behaviors.name: Freeze captures buffer
behaviors.description: Stops buffer recording. Grains continue from whatever material was in the buffer at the moment of freeze. Combined with slow POSITION scan, produces classic infinite-hold ambient effects.

behaviors.name: Audio-rate SIZE modulation
behaviors.description: Modulating grain size at audio rate produces FM-like sidebands and glitchy grain-interruption artifacts. A classic Clouds sound — pair with a slow buffer position for robotic textures.

behaviors.name: In-gain clipping character
behaviors.trigger: IN GAIN past noon with hot source
behaviors.description: The buffer stores at 16-bit 32kHz with a soft-clip limiter on input. Overdriving IN GAIN adds gentle saturation and lo-fi character that is part of the Clouds sound signature.

behaviors.name: Frozen + reverse scrub
behaviors.trigger: Freeze on + slowly rotate POSITION CCW
behaviors.description: With recording frozen, moving POSITION CCW scans backwards through the frozen material. Paired with low DENSITY it turns Clouds into a hand-scrubbable tape machine.

behaviors.name: Parameter persistence across modes
behaviors.description: Each mode stores its own blend-knob assignments and settings. Returning to granular mode restores the granular-mode parameters — useful for A/B-ing modes live.

patchIdeas.name: Frozen ambient pad
patchIdeas.patch: Capture a phrase, freeze the buffer, and drown it in reverb for an instant evolving pad.
steps: Start in Granular mode (default on power-up — LED is green).
Short-press the small Quality/blend-select button until the Blend LED is yellow (Reverb slot).
Turn the Blend knob to ~3 o'clock (heavy reverb tail).
POSITION at noon, SIZE at ~2 o'clock (large grains), DENSITY at ~2 o'clock (thick synchronous cloud), TEXTURE at noon.
Patch your source → IN L (IN R is normalized from L for mono).
Patch OUT L → output L, OUT R → output R.
Play a few seconds of source, then press FREEZE — the button lights, buffer stops recording.
Unpatch the source or leave it — you're now hearing only the frozen buffer reverberated.
patchIdeas.signalOut: OUT L / OUT R — stereo reverberated grain cloud, ±8V, continues forever until you un-freeze.
patchIdeas.hear: A motionless lush pad that shimmers and breathes as grains re-scan the frozen snippet. Nudge POSITION by hand to scrub into different parts of the capture.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Clouds:
* Mode: Granular (green)
* Freeze: ON (after capture)
* Blend slot: Reverb (yellow), ~3 o'clock
* Size: ~2 o'clock · Density: ~2 o'clock

- Source (Out) -> Clouds (IN L)
- Clouds (OUT L) -> Output (L)
- Clouds (OUT R) -> Output (R)

patchIdeas.name: Pitch-shifted looping delay
patchIdeas.patch: Switch to Looping Delay mode so SIZE becomes delay time and PITCH CV detunes each repeat.
steps: Hold FREEZE and short-press the Quality/blend-select button repeatedly until the Blend LED turns red — that's Looping Delay mode. Release FREEZE.
Short-press the Quality button until the Blend LED is orange (Feedback slot), then set the Blend knob to ~2 o'clock for 3–4 audible repeats.
SIZE sets delay time — start at noon (~1s). DENSITY at noon. TEXTURE at noon.
Patch your drum loop → IN L.
Patch a sequencer CV → V/OCT — each clock step transposes the repeats by that interval.
Patch OUT L → output L, OUT R → output R.
Turn SIZE live to slide the delay tape-style.
patchIdeas.signalOut: OUT L / OUT R — dry+delayed drum loop ±8V, each repeat transposed by the V/OCT step.
patchIdeas.hear: A dub-style delay where the echoes climb the sequence. Short SIZE gives tight slapback; long SIZE gives tape scrubbing when you turn it live.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Clouds:
* Mode: Looping Delay (red) // hold FREEZE + tap Quality until red
* Blend slot: Feedback (orange), ~2 o'clock
* Size: noon (~1s)

- Drum Loop (Out) -> Clouds (IN L)
- Sequencer (Pitch) p> Clouds (V/OCT)
- Clouds (OUT L) -> Output (L)
- Clouds (OUT R) -> Output (R)

patchIdeas.name: Audio-rate SIZE FM
patchIdeas.patch: Modulate grain size at audio rate with a VCO to get bell-like sidebands on a frozen chord.
steps: Confirm Granular mode (green Blend LED — power-cycle if unsure).
Short-press the Quality button until the Blend LED is green (Dry/Wet slot), set Blend knob fully CW (all wet).
POSITION at noon, SIZE at ~11 o'clock, DENSITY at ~2 o'clock (thick cloud), TEXTURE at noon.
Patch a held chord or sustained pad → IN L.
Press FREEZE to lock the buffer on the chord.
Patch a VCO square wave (50–500Hz) → SIZE CV, attenuverter at ~2 o'clock.
Patch a slow LFO (cycle ~20s) → POSITION CV, attenuverter at ~1 o'clock.
Patch OUT L → mixer L, OUT R → mixer R.
patchIdeas.signalOut: OUT L / OUT R — frozen chord mangled into FM-metallic tones ±8V, sidebands tracking the VCO.
patchIdeas.hear: The static chord turns into bell-like, clangy partials that shimmer as the VCO pitch changes. Slow POSITION drift keeps the texture from locking — each octave of the modulating VCO is a different bell.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Clouds:
* Mode: Granular
* Freeze: ON
* Blend slot: Dry/Wet (green), full CW

- Held Chord (Out) -> Clouds (IN L)
- VCO (Square) >> Clouds (SIZE CV) // audio-rate
- LFO (Slow) >> Clouds (POSITION CV)
- Clouds (OUT L) -> Mixer (L)
- Clouds (OUT R) -> Mixer (R)

patchIdeas.name: Stretch-drone from a field recording
patchIdeas.patch: Pitch-Shifter / Time-Stretcher mode slows a field recording to a crawl while you pitch it down an octave.
steps: Hold FREEZE and short-press the Quality button until the Blend LED turns yellow (Pitch-Shifter/Time-Stretcher mode). Release FREEZE.
Short-press Quality until the Blend LED is yellow in the Reverb slot, Blend knob to ~2 o'clock for a wet tail.
DENSITY full CCW — in this mode DENSITY is the playback-speed control, so CCW means very slow.
SIZE at ~3 o'clock (huge stretch window), TEXTURE at noon.
PITCH knob CCW one octave (−12 semitones) — in this mode PITCH is decoupled from time.
Patch your field recording → IN L.
Patch OUT L → output L, OUT R → output R.
patchIdeas.signalOut: OUT L / OUT R — time-stretched field recording ±8V, pitched down an octave, with reverb.
patchIdeas.hear: A recognizable source — traffic, wind, voices — slowed to a glacial pad, an octave lower, drowning in its own reverb. Turn DENSITY live to speed the scrub up and down like a tape head.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Clouds:
* Mode: Pitch-Shifter / Time-Stretcher (yellow) // hold FREEZE + tap Quality until yellow
* Pitch: -1 oct
* Density: full CCW // playback speed
* Size: ~3 o'clock
* Blend slot: Reverb, ~2 o'clock

- Field Rec (Out) -> Clouds (IN L)
- Clouds (OUT L) -> Output (L)
- Clouds (OUT R) -> Output (R)

patchIdeas.name: Spectral phaser
patchIdeas.patch: Spectral mode + two slow LFOs on TEXTURE and POSITION turn any sustained source into paul-stretch phaser.
steps: Hold FREEZE and short-press the Quality button until the Blend LED turns pink — that's Spectral Madness. Release FREEZE.
Short-press Quality until Blend LED is green (Dry/Wet), Blend knob full CW (all wet).
POSITION noon, SIZE ~1 o'clock, DENSITY noon, TEXTURE noon as a starting point.
Patch a synth pad → IN L (something sustained — this mode needs steady input).
Patch LFO 1 (cycle ~15s) → TEXTURE CV, attenuverter ~2 o'clock — animates phase randomization.
Patch LFO 2 (cycle ~40s) → POSITION CV, attenuverter ~2 o'clock — sweeps frequency bins.
Patch OUT L → mixer L, OUT R → mixer R.
patchIdeas.signalOut: OUT L / OUT R — spectrally-phased pad ±8V, bins drifting with POSITION, phases smearing with TEXTURE.
patchIdeas.hear: A slow whooshing phaser laid over your pad — not the LFO-on-filter kind, the paul-stretch kind where partials seem to un-glue and float independently. SIZE past 2 o'clock turns it into smeared granular ice.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Clouds:
* Mode: Spectral Madness (pink) // hold FREEZE + tap Quality until pink
* Blend slot: Dry/Wet, full CW

- Pad (Out) -> Clouds (IN L)
- LFO 1 (Slow) >> Clouds (TEXTURE CV)
- LFO 2 (Slower) >> Clouds (POSITION CV)
- Clouds (OUT L) -> Mixer (L)
- Clouds (OUT R) -> Mixer (R)

patchIdeas.name: Trigger-sprayed shimmer grains
patchIdeas.patch: One grain per trigger, pitched up a perfect fifth, drowned in reverb — a classic shimmer sprayer.
steps: Confirm Granular mode (green Blend LED).
Short-press Quality until Blend LED is yellow (Reverb slot), Blend knob full CW.
DENSITY full CCW — at this extreme new grains only spawn on an external TRIG pulse.
SIZE at ~1 o'clock (medium-long grains), TEXTURE at noon.
PITCH knob CW to +7 semitones (reads on the display as +7) for a fifth shimmer.
Patch your source (pad, vocal, guitar) → IN L.
Patch a rhythmic clock or gate → TRIG — each pulse fires one pitched grain.
Patch OUT L → output L, OUT R → output R.
patchIdeas.signalOut: OUT L / OUT R — one pitched grain per trigger ±8V, drenched in reverb, dry source still audible via the wet/dry mix.
patchIdeas.hear: Each trigger sprays a bright fifth up into a cathedral. At faster clocks it becomes a shimmering harp; at 1/4-note clocks it sits like a glassy counter-melody.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Clouds:
* Mode: Granular (green)
* Density: full CCW // 1 grain per TRIG
* Pitch: +7 semitones
* Blend slot: Reverb (yellow), full CW

- Source (Out) -> Clouds (IN L)
- Clock (Out) t> Clouds (TRIG)
- Clouds (OUT L) -> Output (L)
- Clouds (OUT R) -> Output (R)

patchIdeas.name: Feedback runaway drone
patchIdeas.patch: Crank the Feedback blend slot near runaway and let a short capture regenerate itself into an infinite drone.
steps: Granular mode (green).
Short-press Quality until the Blend LED is orange (Feedback slot), Blend knob at ~3 o'clock — just shy of runaway.
POSITION noon, SIZE ~1 o'clock, DENSITY ~2 o'clock, TEXTURE ~1 o'clock.
Patch a source → IN L, play a few seconds of interesting material.
Press FREEZE — the buffer locks.
Unpatch IN L. The output now feeds itself through the feedback path.
Patch OUT L → mixer L, OUT R → mixer R.
Nudge Blend CCW if it screams; nudge CW to revive it.
patchIdeas.signalOut: OUT L / OUT R — self-regenerating grain drone ±8V, slowly mutating from the original capture.
patchIdeas.hear: A drone that started as your source but gradually smears into harmonic mush, then back into something vocal, then metallic — a slow hallucination. Texture and Size sculpt its character live.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Clouds:
* Mode: Granular
* Freeze: ON
* Blend slot: Feedback (orange), ~3 o'clock

- Source (Out) -> Clouds (IN L) // briefly, then unpatch
- Clouds (OUT L) -> Mixer (L)
- Clouds (OUT R) -> Mixer (R)

patchIdeas.name: Reverse-scrub tape effect
patchIdeas.patch: Capture a melodic phrase, freeze it, then drive POSITION with a ramp-down LFO to scrub backwards.
steps: Granular mode (green).
Short-press Quality until Blend LED is green (Dry/Wet), Blend knob full CW (all wet — we only want the reversed grains).
Patch a melodic source → IN L. Play the phrase.
Press FREEZE the moment the phrase ends — buffer holds.
SIZE at ~11 o'clock (small-medium grains), DENSITY at ~3 o'clock (dense overlap), TEXTURE at ~1 o'clock.
Patch a slow ramp-DOWN LFO (cycle ~6s) → POSITION CV, turn the POSITION attenuverter full CW to maximum positive.
A descending ramp across the buffer plays it backwards.
Patch OUT L → output L, OUT R → output R.
patchIdeas.signalOut: OUT L / OUT R — reversed rendition of the captured phrase ±8V, looping with the LFO.
patchIdeas.hear: Your phrase plays backwards, like a reversed tape — swells into notes instead of out of them. Speed up the LFO to chop it into glitchy reverse-stutters; slow it down for ambient backwards swell.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Clouds:
* Mode: Granular
* Freeze: ON (after capture)
* Blend slot: Dry/Wet, full CW

- Source (Phrase) -> Clouds (IN L) // capture then freeze
- LFO (Ramp Down) >> Clouds (POSITION CV) // attenuverter max CW
- Clouds (OUT L) -> Output (L)
- Clouds (OUT R) -> Output (R)
